Japanese: １２月２９日　午前　９時４３分 地方裁判所　被告人第５控え室
English: December 29, 9:43 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 5

Japanese: おはようございます。
English: Good morning Max.

Japanese: マックスさん？
English: Max?

Japanese: ‥‥ぎゅ‥‥牛乳。
English: Mil... Milk...

Japanese: え？
English: What?

Japanese: ス‥‥ステージに上がる前に 牛乳を飲まないと‥‥
English: If I don't have a glass of milk before I go on stage...

Japanese: お、落ちつかないんだよハニイ。
English: I just can't function sweetie.

Japanese: す、すてーじ‥‥
English: St-Stage?

Japanese: だいじょうぶですよ。今日は 座ってるだけでいいんですから。
English: Don't worry... There won't be a stage. All you have to do is sit down.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ま、まあね。
English: I guess...

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick.

Japanese: マックスさん、 すごくキンチョーしてるよ。
English: Max is really nervous.

Japanese: ‥‥ムリもないけどね。
English: That's understandable.

Japanese: なあ！　ハニイたち！
English: Hey! My sweeties!!

Japanese: な、なんですか！
English: W-What!?

Japanese: ‥‥ボク、飛ぼうか？
English: You don't think I should fly, do you?

Japanese: ハイ？
English: Huh?

Japanese: いや。こういうのは、最初が カンジンなんだよ。
English: You know... You've got to make a good first impression!

Japanese: 登場したときに、客の心をまず、 ガッチリつかまないと！
English: When I enter the room, maybe I should fly in and warm up the crowd a little...

Japanese: い、いやいやいや。
English: No no no no no...

Japanese: 被告が法廷を飛び回って どうするんですか！
English: We can't be having you flying around the courtroom... It just wouldn't be right.

Japanese: フザけてると 思われちゃいますよ！
English: Imagine if you hit someone!

Japanese: だいじょうぶですよ。 なるほどくんにまかせて‥‥
English: Don't worry Max... Just do what Nick says and everything will be OK.

Japanese: なあ！　ハニイ！
English: Oh sweetie!!

Japanese: こ、今度はなんですか？
English: What is it this time, Max?

Japanese: アンタ、飛んでみないか！
English: Why don't you try flying into the courtroom?

Japanese: 弁護士が空から入場！
English: I can see it now... The dashing young lawyer flying fabulously in from above!

Japanese: これだけで、 客はハニイにクギづけになるよ！
English: One glimpse of that, and everyone in the room will be on your side!

Japanese: だ、だからマックスさん。 だいじょうぶですから‥‥。
English: Max... Really... No one needs to fly today.

Japanese: ‥‥？ なるほどくん？
English: Nick? What's with that look in your eyes?

Japanese: （‥‥ちょっと、飛んでみたい）
English: (I like the sound of that... "Dashing young lawyer flying fabulously...")

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第２法廷
English: December 29, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 2
And now the case of one...
What!?

Japanese: のっけからナニ！　裁判長！
English: Your Honor! Get on with it!

Japanese: い、いや。被告人の名前が “山田　耕平”とあるもので‥‥。
English: Oh... Sorry... I just realized that the defendant's name is Billy Bob Johns.

Japanese: それが？
English: So...

Japanese: でも、被告はあの‥‥マックス・ ギャラクティカなワケですよね？
English: Well, isn't the defendant also known as Maximillion Galactica?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、一般的には。
English: Yes, Your Honor, he does often go by that name.

Japanese: じつは、マゴが大ファンでしてね。
English: You know, my grandchild is a huge fan of his.

Japanese: ‥‥みなさんも、そっちのほうが 親しみやすいでしょうから。
English: I think everyone here wouldn't mind if we call the defendant Maximillion Galactica...

Japanese: これよりマックス・ギャラクティカ の法廷を開廷します。
English: It sounds more... Friendly.

Japanese: （いいのか‥‥？）
English: (Hmm... I wonder if that is to our advantage?)

Japanese: 狩魔検事。 冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: Ms. von Karma. Your opening statement if you please.

Japanese: ‥‥勝ったと思わないコトね 成歩堂　龍一‥‥。
English: ...I hope you didn't bother thinking you'd win this one Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: え。
English: Eh?

Japanese: 霊媒による殺人なんて、 法律で認められていないわ。
English: That spirit channeling trial was a sham! I refuse to acknowledge its legitimacy!

Japanese: あんな裁判は茶番。 いわばノーカウントよ！
English: It did not count! Do you hear me!?

Japanese: （‥‥ま、まだ前回の事件に こだわってるみたいだな‥‥）
English: (She must still be upset about what happened last time...)

Japanese: 私はゼッタイに負けるワケには いかない！
English: You have no chance. Zero. Zilch. Nada. I'm not losing this case!

Japanese: なぜなら、狩魔の人間は常に カンペキでなければならないから！
English: Why, you ask? Because it is not in the nature of a Von Karma to lose at anything!!

Japanese: 狩魔のニンゲンは、ホント シツコイねえ、なるほどくん。
English: I guess being born with the name Von Karma is a free pass to be arrogant and annoying.

Japanese: 見てなさい成歩堂　龍一！
English: Watch and learn, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: 今度こそ、キサマを有罪に してみせる！
English: I'll show you the textbook procedure for proving how absolutely guilty you are!!

Japanese: ぼぼ、ぼくを有罪ですか‥‥？
English: M-M-M-Me!? Guilty!?

Japanese: あいた！
English: What are you talking about!?

Japanese: ‥‥それが、私の “復讐”なのよ‥‥
English: ...It will be my ultimate revenge...

Japanese: 狩魔検事のおとうさん‥‥ もう、いないのにね。
English: Her dad is gone you know...

Japanese: ‥‥以上。 冒頭弁論を終了する。
English: The prosecution is finished. Let's hurry and wrap up this waste of time.

Japanese: では。最初の証人を 呼んでいただきましょう。
English: You may call your first witness Ms. von Karma.

Japanese: イトノコギリ刑事を 入廷させなさい！
English: Detective Dick Gumshoe! Get up there! NOW!!

Japanese: 給料も安いのに、 今日もごくろう、刑事。
English: Sorry to keep you from work, as I'm sure you need every penny you can earn, Detective.

Japanese: トンでもないッス！ 自分はヤル気マンマンッス！
English: Don't mention it! It's no trouble at all! I've been looking forward to this.

Japanese: じゃあさっそく、事件の流れを 説明しなさい。
English: Very well. I would like you to begin by shedding light on the events in question.

Japanese: オッケッス！
English: At your service, sir.

Japanese: では、刑事。 証言をおねがいします。
English: Alright, Detective. You may proceed with your testimony.

Japanese: ～事件の流れについて～
English: -- Details of the Events --

Japanese: 事件当夜は、９時４０分まで雪が ふって、とてもサムかったッス。
English: The night of the crime, snow was falling until 9:40 PM, making it extremely cold out.

Japanese: あの夜は、全員がテントに集まって 演技の練習をしていたッス。
English: All of the circus performers gathered in the Big Top to practice their routines.

Japanese: 練習は午後１０時に終わり、 カイサンになったッス。
English: The practice session broke up around 10 PM.

Japanese: 事件は１０時１５分、 宿舎前の広場で起こったッス。
English: The murder itself took place in the plaza in front of the lodging house at 10:15 PM.

Japanese: 被害者は、大きな木箱の上に 突っぷして死んでいたッス。
English: The victim was found bent over a wooden box dead as a doornail.

Japanese: 死因は、強く殴られた際の クビの骨折ッス。
English: The cause of death was blunt force trauma that snapped a vertebrae in his neck.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ 撲殺、というわけですか。
English: I see... He was beaten to death.

Japanese: これが被害者の解剖記録ッス。
English: Here is the autopsy report for the victim.

Japanese: 受理します。
English: The court accept this into evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《解剖記録》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Autopsy Report added to the Court Record.

Japanese: “平たい”鈍器‥‥ですか。 ふむう‥‥
English: A blunt object... Hmm...

Japanese: では、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Very well... Mr. Wright, you may begin your cross-examination.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～事件の流れについて～
English: -- Details of the Events --

Japanese: 事件当夜は、９時４０分まで雪が ふって、とてもサムかったッス。
English: The night of the crime, snow was falling until 9:40 PM, making it extremely cold out.

Japanese: 事件当夜、全員がテントに集まって 演技の練習があったッス。
English: All of the circus performers gathered in the Big Top to practice their routines.

Japanese: 練習は午後１０時に終わり、 カイサンになったッス。
English: The practice session broke up around 10 PM.

Japanese: 事件は１０時１５分、 宿舎前の広場で起こったッス。
English: The murder itself took place in the plaza in front of the lodging house at 10:15 PM.

Japanese: 被害者は、大きな木箱の上に 突っぷして死んでいたッス。
English: The victim was found bent over a wooden box dead as a doornail.

Japanese: 死因は、強く殴られた際の クビの骨折ッス。
English: The cause of death was blunt force trauma that snapped a vertebrae in his neck.

Japanese: ‥‥今日のイトノコさん、 ツッコミどころがないね。
English: There wasn't much that we could go after Gumshoe with this time...

Japanese: 必要な情報だけもらって、 サッサとお帰りいただこうか。
English: He gave us the facts, as he saw them. But there's gotta be more to this.

Japanese: ザンネンだね。
English: I didn't see anything...

Japanese: さっき“ヤル気マンマンッス” って言ってたのに。
English: But that's why you're the lawyer and I'm the medium.

Japanese: 雪が‥‥？
English: Let me ask you about the snow...

Japanese: まあ、事件の前までには やんでいたッスが。
English: It was nearly a blizzard up until the time of the crime.

Japanese: つもっていましたか？
English: Did it pile up?

Japanese: 大したことはなかったッス。 うっすら３センチぐらいッスかね。
English: It wasn't such a big deal... Maybe about an inch and a half was on the ground.

Japanese: 雪はそのままこおりついて、 次の日にも残っていたッス。
English: The snow froze in place and stayed on the ground until the next day.

Japanese: （雪‥‥か。いずれまた、 モンダイになるんだろうな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... The snow... Let me see... There's got to be more to this...)

Japanese: え。どうして？　なるほどくん。
English: Eh? What's the matter? Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥証拠品を見てみなよ。
English: I need to take a look at the Court Record...

Japanese: 証人。その夜、サーカスの 人たちは、何を‥‥？
English: Mr. Gumshoe, what were the members of the circus doing on the night of the crime?

Japanese: “全員”というのは？
English: When you say "all of the circus performers"... Who do you mean?

Japanese: スタッフやダンサーをのぞいた 主役級の芸人たちッス。
English: Everyone but the dancers and staff were there.

Japanese: 猛獣使いのミリカさん、ピエロの トミー、腹話術師のベンさん‥‥
English: Regina the animal tamer, Moe the clown, Ben the Ventriloquist...

Japanese: 被告のマックス・ギャラクティカに ‥‥もちろん、団長自身ッス。
English: And of course, the defendant, Maximillion Galactica, and his victim, the Ringmaster.

Japanese: ついでに、トラのラトーも いたッス。
English: Oh, I almost forgot... Regent the tiger was there as well.

Japanese: ちなみに、あのサルは？
English: Out of curiosity, what about the circus monkey?

Japanese: きのう、捜査中に ウデ時計を取られたッス。
English: When I was investigating yesterday, he happened to snatch my wristwatch.

Japanese: それはカンケイないでしょう！
English: Detective, you are welcome to file a police report AFTER these proceedings!

Japanese: カイサンしたあと、 みなさんはどちらへ‥‥？
English: After the practice was over, where did everyone head off to...?

Japanese: ミリカさんはラトーとたわむれ、 ピエロはつかれきって宿舎へ。
English: Regina was playing with Regent, while Moe went back to his room tired from work...

Japanese: 腹話術師は正門前で モノ思いにふけっていたそうッス。
English: Ben the ventriloquist went to the front gate absorbed in his own world...

Japanese: 団長とマックスは、２人で 団長室で密談を。
English: The Ringmaster and Max went off to the Ringmaster's room to talk privately.

Japanese: ミツダン‥‥ それはアヤシイですね‥‥。
English: Talk privately, huh? That's awfully suspicious...

Japanese: いったい、ナニを 話していたのですか？
English: You wouldn't happen to know what they were talking about would you?

Japanese: ギャラの交渉を したらしいッス。
English: It seems they were negotiating Max's salary...

Japanese: （ウソつけ。“お嬢さんを ください”ってやってたくせに）
English: (Actually, Max was asking for Regina's hand in marriage!)

Japanese: １０時１５分‥‥ ハッキリしてますね。 10:15 PM.
English: I'd like you to be a bit more specific about the events at

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥ モチロンッス。
English: Uh... Umm... OK... Not a problem, pal.

Japanese: なにしろこちらには、 バッチリ証人がいる
English: We've got a witness that told us how the whole thing went down.

Japanese: うはっ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥手のウチをあかす バカがどこにいる‥‥。
English: This is totally meaningless. Time to move on!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥では、それは お楽しみにとっておくとして、
English: Hmm... Alright... We'll just have to revisit that testimony later.

Japanese: 証人。被害者はどうやって 亡くなったのですかな？
English: Detective Gumshoe, would you mind telling us how the victim met his end?

Japanese: “木箱”‥‥ですか？
English: A "wooden box"?

Japanese: そッス。
English: That's right.

Japanese: なぜか被害者は、大きな木箱を 抱きかかえて死んでいたッス。
English: The victim must have been carrying the wooden box when he was killed.

Japanese: 抱きかかえて‥‥
English: Carrying the box, huh?

Japanese: ちょっと変わった 木箱だったッス。
English: It was a rather strange wooden box, Your Honor.

Japanese: どういうことですかな？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 見かけのワリには妙に重くて ‥‥そして、カギがかかっていて。
English: Well... It was much heavier than it looked... Not to mention it was locked.

Japanese: カギ、ですか‥‥。
English: Locked, you say?

Japanese: 証拠品《木箱》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Wooden Box added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （せっかくだから、なんか 聞いてみるか‥‥）
English: (This may be my only chance, so I might as well ask some questions...)

Japanese: その木箱ですけど‥‥ １０キロもあったんですか？
English: This wooden box... You figure it weighs about 20 pounds, right?

Japanese: そッスねー。箱の中に 鉄板が張ってあったッス。
English: Sounds about right. The whole thing is lined with iron.

Japanese: てっぱん‥‥ また、なぜですか？
English: Iron plating? Why do you think it's lined with iron?

Japanese: そりゃまあやっぱり、 箱を重くするためかなと
English: I figure they just did it to make the box heavier...

Japanese: むぎッ！
English: Yeowch!!

Japanese: ‥‥スイソクなど 聞いていない。
English: I think we could all do without your guesses, Detective.

Japanese: 知ってることだけ答えなさい。
English: Stick to what little you actually know!

Japanese: す‥‥すまねッス‥‥。
English: S-S-Sorry...

Japanese: と、とにかく、ズッシリした 箱だったッス。
English: Suffice it to say, the box is really heavy.

Japanese: それでいいわ。 ‥‥先をつづけて、刑事。
English: Much better. You may continue on, Detective.

Japanese: 木箱にかかってたカギは どういう‥‥？
English: You said the box was locked. How exactly was it locked?

Japanese: そッスねー‥‥
English: Well...

Japanese: ちっちゃいけど、 ガンジョウそうなシロモノッス。
English: It had quite a sturdy lock on it. It took quite a bit to open that bad boy up.

Japanese: 発見されたときに、 そのカギは‥‥？
English: So, when you found the box, it was locked, correct?

Japanese: かかってたッス。
English: Yes, it was.

Japanese: 鑑識の連中が ぶっコワしたッス。
English: Some of the forensics team took it back to the precinct to crack it open.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥。 では、証言をつづけてください。
English: I see... Continue Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: ‥‥その箱の中には、 何が入っていたんですか？
English: Do you mind telling us what was inside that box?

Japanese: ワレワレが発見したとき、 木箱にはカギがかかっていたッス。
English: Well, when we found the box, it was locked tighter than Fort Knox.

Japanese: 署でぶっコワしたッスが‥‥
English: So we took it back to the station and cracked it open.

Japanese: 中には‥‥この小さなビンが 入っていただけッス。
English: All that was inside was this little bottle...

Japanese: ビン‥‥？ 刑事。なんですか、それ。
English: Bottle? What is that, Detective?

Japanese: 見たとおり‥‥ 調味料のビン、ッスね。
English: Exactly what it looks like Your Honor. It's a condiment bottle.

Japanese: ビンの中身はなんですか？
English: What's inside the bottle?

Japanese: コショウ‥‥ッス。
English: It's filled with... Pepper.

Japanese: コショウ‥‥？ どうして、そんなものが？
English: Pepper...? Why in the world was it locked in that big box?

Japanese: （あんな大きな箱に、 小さなビンが１個だけ‥‥）
English: (There was only one little bottle in that huge box...?)

Japanese: なんか、イミあるのかなあ‥‥？
English: I wonder if that has some sort of special meaning?

Japanese: 証拠品《小ビン》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Small Seasoning Bottle added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 解剖記録によると、凶器は “平たい鈍器”ということですね？
English: According to the Autopsy Report, the murder weapon was a blunt object, correct?

Japanese: そッスね。
English: You've done your homework, pal.

Japanese: 凶器自体は 見つかってないんですか？
English: And you haven't found this murder weapon, have you?

Japanese: 今、全力をあげて捜索中よ。
English: The police are searching for it as we speak.

Japanese: おそらく、犯人が持ち去った ものと考えられるッス。
English: My theory is that it is something the perpetrator ran off with.

Japanese: そりゃそうでしょう。 現場にないんですから。
English: You would think so... Especially since you didn't find it on the scene.

Japanese: いやいや。もしかしたら、 魔術で消したのかも。クックッ‥‥
English: No no no... I bet he made it disappear with magic... Ho ho ho!

Japanese: ‥‥事件の流れは だいたいわかりました。
English: Well, I think we have a good feel for the details of the event now.

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事から引き出せる 情報は、こんなものかな‥‥）
English: (I guess that's all we're gonna get out of Gumshoe in this case...)

Japanese: “こんなもの”、って‥‥ コショウのビンだけだよ？
English: You mean all we're gonna get out of him is that little bottle of pepper?

Japanese: ではここで、次の証人を 呼びたいと思う。
English: Now that we have wrapped up with the Detective, I'd like to call my next witness.

Japanese: えっ！　自分はもう、 おしまいッスか？
English: Eh? I'm not even off the stand yet!

Japanese: ‥‥そう。アナタはもう、 オシマイよ。
English: Obviously... But that's due to you being slow and unable to take a hint!

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、今の“オシマイ”、 いやーなヒビキが‥‥
English: I dunno, but "wrapped up" has such a mean sound to it... I'm a sensitive guy...

Japanese: ‥‥イトノコ刑事。 ごくろうさまでした。
English: Thank you very much, Detective Gumshoe. You may step down.

Japanese: それでは狩魔検事。 次の証人を。
English: Ms. von Karma. Call your next witness.

Japanese: 腹話術師の木住　勉を 入廷させなさい。
English: I would like to call Mr. Benjamin Woodman to the stand.

Japanese: （ベンさん、てコトは‥‥）
English: (She must be talking about Ben the ventriloquist...)

Japanese: リロくんも出てくるのかな。 やっぱり‥‥。
English: I wonder if Trilo will show up on the stand as well...?

Japanese: 証人。‥‥名前と職業を。
English: Please state your name and occupation for the record.

Japanese: 名前はリロくん。 職業はテノール歌手だ。
English: My full name is Trilo Quist. I am employed as an opera tenor.

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ちなさい。
English: Ex-Excuse me!

Japanese: 証人の名前は木住　勉、 職業は腹話術師のハズでは‥‥
English: The witness called to the stand was one Mr. Benjamin Woodman, Ventriloquist.

Japanese: ダレのコトだよ！ 歌手だって言ってるだろうが！
English: That robe must be cutting off your circulation. I said that I was a singer!!

Japanese: そうだな、１曲歌っちゃうか。
English: Maybe you don't believe me. Fine. I'll grace you with a song.

Japanese: ‥‥エヘン！
English: *Ahem*... Mi mi mi mi mi!

Japanese: 裁判サンバは～ココロもはずむ～ ユーザイムザイか～お楽しみぃ～
English: The world of the law... Exciting and daring... Guilt or innocence...

Japanese: ‥‥ってかァ！
English: Decided by a judge dressed up like a woman!! ...Well, what do you think?

Japanese: 今度はリズムがいいねえ。
English: It had a good rhythm...

Japanese: あいかわらず 歌詞はサイアクだけどな。
English: It's just the lyrics... They leave something to be desired, so to speak.

Japanese: ダ‥‥ダメだよリロくん。 こんなところで‥‥
English: T-Trilo! You know better than to insult a judge!!

Japanese: バカだな。こういうのはなァ、 ツカミがダイジなんだよ！
English: Shuddup! Just look at your nose. You would think you'd have the sense to fix it!!

Japanese: 登場したら、まず１発カマす！
English: It's so ugly I want to punch you in the face on the off chance swelling would help.

Japanese: ‥‥ったく、そんなことだから いつまでも二流芸人なんだよ！
English: You know that your nose is the reason you'll never be an A-List star.

Japanese: 芸人って、みんな似たような コト、考えてるんだね。
English: Celebrities must really enjoy saying everything that flashes into their minds.

Japanese: ‥‥しょ‥‥証人！ い、今のは‥‥？
English: What's going on here!? Order! Order! I demand to know who the witness is!!

Japanese: あ、アノ。あまりその、 ボクのコトは、気になさらず‥‥
English: Don't... Don't worry about me, sir. I'll let Trilo handle this.

Japanese: し、しかしそんなコト 言いましてもアナ
English: I'm not worried about you one bit... I'm worried about getting testimony in my court!

Japanese: ふひぎッ！
English: OUCH!!

Japanese: ‥‥証人の事情はセンサク無用。 さっそく証言をしてもらうわ。
English: You won't get anywhere trying to figure out this witness! Now let's proceed!

Japanese: ～事件の当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- What You Witnessed --

Japanese: 練習が終わって、あのピエロと テントを出たんだよ。
English: Once practice was over, I left the tent with the stooge... I mean clown.

Japanese: 宿舎前でアイツとわかれて、その あとは広場の入り口にいたんだ。
English: Once we got to the lodging house, I ditched him and went over to the plaza.

Japanese: そしたらさ。目の前をマックスが 通って、広場へ向かったんだよ。
English: That's when I saw Max heading towards the scene of the crime.

Japanese: 通ったのはアイツだけだぜ。 これで犯人はキマリだよな！
English: He was the only one heading that way... How could that punk not be the killer!?

Japanese: そのうち、警察が来てさ。 それでオレも、事件を知ったワケ。
English: Then the police showed up, and took magic boy away.

Japanese: マックス・ギャラクティカが 目の前を通った。まちがいない？
English: You saw Maximillion Galactica heading towards the scene? You're sure of that?

Japanese: モチロン！　シルクハットにマント そしてムネの白いバラ！
English: Without a doubt. He had on his silk hat, cloak, and the dumb white roses on his chest.

Japanese: イヤミな３点セットをぶら下げて、 鼻先を通りすぎやがったんだ！
English: How can you mistake someone with that crazy get-up and his nose stuck up so high...

Japanese: ちょ‥‥、 ちょっと待ってください！
English: Th-That's enough... I think we all get the picture.

Japanese: “ピエロといっしょに” テントを出たのですよね？
English: Just one thing... You said you "ditched" the clown.

Japanese: そうだぜ。
English: That's right, dress-boy.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥そのピエロさんも アヤシイではないですか！
English: Well, since you weren't with him, couldn't that mean the clown committed the crime?

Japanese: （あ。たしかに‥‥）
English: (Hmm... He's got a point.)

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンながら‥‥ ピエロは犯人ではあり得ないわ。
English: What a shame... It was a nice theory, but the clown can't be the criminal.

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか？
English: Why's that?

Japanese: これがその証拠よ。
English: Here's absolute proof.

Japanese: それは‥‥ シルクハット、ですか。
English: A... silk hat?

Japanese: 殺人現場から発見されたわ。 ‥‥被告人のものよ。
English: This was found at the scene of the crime. It belongs to the defendant.
Ah...

Japanese: 練習のとき、被告人はモチロン、 これをアタマに乗せていたわ。
English: Without question, he was wearing his signature hat during practice.

Japanese: ピエロが犯人なら、 現場に落ちているはずがない。
English: If the clown was the murderer, there would be no reason for this hat to be at the scene.

Japanese: ‥‥ふ、ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: さすが狩魔検事‥‥ 立証はカンペキですな。
English: Well done, Ms. von Karma. Your prosecutorial skills are unrivaled. Well done.

Japanese: ‥‥当然。 じゃ、あとはよろしく。
English: Thank you for stating the obvious... Mr. Phoenix Wright, what do you have to say?

Japanese: ハイ‥‥ （今日もヤツのペースだな‥‥）
English: O-OK... (I guess she's the boss again today...)

Japanese: ～事件の当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- What You Witnessed --

Japanese: 練習が終わって、あのピエロと テントを出たんだよ。
English: Once practice was over, I left the tent with the stooge... I mean clown.

Japanese: 宿舎前でアイツとわかれて、その あとは広場の入り口にいたんだ。
English: Once we got to the lodging house, I ditched him and went over to the plaza.

Japanese: そしたらさ。目の前をマックスが 通って、広場へ向かったんだよ。
English: That's when I saw Max heading towards the scene of the crime.

Japanese: 通ったのはアイツだけだぜ。 これで犯人はキマリだよな！
English: He was the only one heading that way... How could that punk not be the killer!?

Japanese: そのうち、警察が来てさ。 それでオレも、事件を知ったワケ。
English: Then the police showed up, and took magic boy away.

Japanese: リロくん‥‥、マックスに ウラミでもあるのかな？
English: Hmm... Trilo wouldn't happen to have an ulterior motive for incriminating Max, would he?

Japanese: 恋ガタキだからね。 ミリカちゃんをめぐって。
English: Well, Max is part of that bitter love triangle with Regina.

Japanese: それに、マックスに アタマもなぐられてるし。
English: Which is probably why Max conked him over the head.

Japanese: え？　でも、それは ベンさんのほうでしょ？
English: Umm Nick... Wasn't Ben the one who got knocked over the head?

Japanese: ‥‥うう‥‥ もう、ワケわかんないな。
English: Uh... Yeah... I think so... I dunno anymore!

Japanese: “ピエロ”‥‥ トミーさんのことですね？
English: The clown... You're talking about Moe?

Japanese: あのオッサン、トシだから。 見ててイタイタしいぜ。
English: Of course I'm talking about that old fart. He's so pathetic, I can't stand him!

Japanese: ちょっと走りまわっただけで、もう 化粧がズルズルになっちゃって。
English: Just a little bit of exercise and his makeup is running all over the place...

Japanese: 練習が終わったころにゃァ、 ９割がた死んでたね。
English: Once practice was over, he was 9/10ths of the way to keeling over for good...

Japanese: はあ‥‥
English: Poor guy...

Japanese: しかたないから、この ベンが送ってやったワケだ。
English: We didn't have any choice, so Ben took him back to his room.

Japanese: そういうビンボークジ運なら コイツ、世界一だから。
English: When it comes to being a first place loser, that guy's ahead of the pack!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥それから？
English: Hmm... Then what happened?

Japanese: 何をしていたんですか？ そんなところで‥‥。
English: What did you do then?

Japanese: 決まってんだろ！ 考えゴトだよ！
English: I went over to the Plaza to do some thinking.

Japanese: あの夜は雪もふったあとで、 とてもサムかった。
English: It was awfully cold out that night... Especially with all the snow around...

Japanese: 考えゴトなら、宿舎に 帰ってもよかったのでは？
English: Wouldn't thinking in your nice warm lodging house have been a better idea?

Japanese: ‥‥どこで考えゴトをしようが、 証人の勝手。
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright. I think you should leave the thinking to the witness.

Japanese: （ホントに、ただ考えゴトを していたのか‥‥？）
English: (But I'm a good thunker! At least my teachers always said I was...)

Japanese: 本当にマックス・ギャラクティカ だったんですか！
English: Are you sure it was really Max Galactica?

Japanese: あのキザったらしい３点セット、 見まちがえようがねえよ。
English: Of course I'm sure. How could you mistake someone wearing such a snobby 3 piece get-up!

Japanese: キザな３点セット？
English: Snobby 3 piece get-up?

Japanese: やれやれ。最近のベンゴシは なんにも知らねえみたいだな。
English: Get the wax out of your ears! Lawyers nowadays... You're like talking to a brick wall!

Japanese: マックスの３点セット。 さあ、みなさんごいっしょに！
English: Max's 3 piece get-up! Jeez! Could you be any more dense! All together now!

Japanese: “シルクハット！　マント！ 白いバラ！”
English: "SILK HAT! CLOAK! WHITE ROSES!"

Japanese: ‥‥センキウ！
English: Thank you!

Japanese: ベンキョウ不足だね、 なるほどくん！
English: Nick, I think you should put a little bit more effort into preparing your questions...

Japanese: “アイツ”‥‥マックスだけ、 ですか‥‥？
English: You saw Max and only Max, right Trilo?

Japanese: そうさ。 これでハンニンはキマリだよな！
English: That's right. And that makes him the killer!

Japanese: たった１人しか 通らなかったんだからな！
English: There was only one person headed that way that night.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そうですねえ。 たしかに、アヤシイ‥‥。
English: Hmm... That makes quite a bit of sense and makes Max one suspicious character...

Japanese: （裁判長がナットクしている‥‥ ケチでもつけてみるか？）
English: (There's more to this story than meets the eye... Is there something amiss in this?)

Japanese: （ケチをつけようにも、 ネタがないからな‥‥）
English: (I can't really see anything wrong with what Ben... I mean Trilo just said.)

Japanese: ここは、ようすを見ようよ なるほどくん。
English: Maybe you should take a step back and look things over again, Nick.

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Wait a second!

Japanese: 証人が見たのがマックスか どうか、証明されたわけでは‥‥
English: There's no proof that the witness saw Maximillion Galactica...

Japanese: オイオイ。まーだ わかってねえのかベンゴシくん。
English: Hey! Hey! You still don't get it, do ya?

Japanese: オレは見たんだって。 マックスの３点セットをさ。
English: I saw what I saw... It was Max's 3 piece joke of a costume!

Japanese: ハイ、みなさんごいっしょに！
English: Alright! Lets say it all together now!

Japanese: “シルクハット！　マント！ 白いバラ！”
English: "SILK HAT! CLOAK! WHITE ROSES!"

Japanese: ‥‥センキウ！
English: Thank you!

Japanese: ベンキョウ不足ですね、 弁護人！
English: I think you should study up on your celebrities, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （ヘタなツッコミ、 いれるんじゃなかった‥‥）
English: (Great, I'm getting shut down by a toy!)

Japanese: おかしいですね‥‥。
English: That's a bit strange, don't you think?

Japanese: ナニが！
English: What's strange!?

Japanese: マックス１人しか 見なかった‥‥。
English: That you only saw Max...

Japanese: そんなハズはないんですよ。
English: Doesn't it seem like you should have seen someone else as well?

Japanese: そ‥‥それならば お聞きしましょう！
English: What? Where are you going with this, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: この証人が、被告人以外のだれを 見ているはずだと言うのですか！
English: Who else do you suppose this witness could have seen?

Japanese: ダマってんじゃねえよベン！
English: Don't hold your tongue Ben!

Japanese: くだらねえ質問には、 オマエが答えるんだ！
English: It's your job to answer all the stupid questions.

Japanese: ゴ、ゴメンよリロくん‥‥。
English: I'm sorry Trilo...

Japanese: （どうやら、ハズしたみたいだな）
English: (I've gotta find a way to get more information out of this witness...)

Japanese: もう一度、考えようよ！
English: Think about it some more Nick! I'm sure you'll come up with something!!

Japanese: それは‥‥被害者‥‥。
English: That's the... victim...

Japanese: そうです。
English: That's correct.

Japanese: ずっと広場の入り口に いたのならば‥‥
English: If Trilo was at the entrance to the plaza...

Japanese: 現場へ向かう 立見団長を見たはずです！
English: He should have seen the Ringmaster as well!

Japanese: あたッ！
English: Ah-hah!

Japanese: ‥‥立見団長は、証人より早く すでに現場に行っていた。
English: Obviously the Ringmaster arrived at the scene of the crime before the witness

Japanese: そうに決まってるでしょう！
English: could have seen him! Anyone with sense could have figured that one out!

Japanese: ナニを言ってるんですか！
English: What are you talking about!?

Japanese: 団長は、マックスと 団長室で会っていたはずです！
English: The Ringmaster and Max went together to the Ringmaster's room!

Japanese: それは、被告人の勝手な 言いぶんでしょ？
English: Is that according to the defendant? A likely story!

Japanese: ジッサイ、団長室にいるはずの マックス・ギャラクティカは‥‥
English: If Maximillion Galactica was supposed to be in the Ringmaster's room,

Japanese: このとおり、証人に 目撃されているじゃない！
English: why was he, just as the witness stated... At the scene of the crime!?

Japanese: うう‥‥！
English: Ahh!!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、今の段階で 証言をうたがう理由はありません。
English: It seems that at this stage I have no reason to doubt this witness' testimony.

Japanese: 決定的なムジュン、という ワケでもないようです。
English: And there are clearly no conclusive contradictions.

Japanese: そんな‥‥
English: He's right...

Japanese: そのとおり。‥‥気にせず、 証言をつづけなさい。
English: A brilliant judgment Your Honor. Now let's move along with the testimony!

Japanese: “アイツ”‥‥マックスだけ、 ですか‥‥？
English: You saw Max and only Max that night?

Japanese: ウルセエなあ。なんと言われても コタエはおんなじなの！
English: Jeez, you're persistent! You can ask all you want, my answer won't change!

Japanese: オレはマックス１人しか 見てねえんだよお！
English: The only person I saw that night was Maximillion Galactica!

Japanese: （タイド悪いな‥‥）
English: (He really needs to improve his manners...)

Japanese: ‥‥そういうコト。 つづけて、証人。
English: You've heard what he has to say. Let him continue with his testimony!

Japanese: 警察が来たのは 何時ぐらいでしたか？
English: Around what time did the police arrive at the scene?

Japanese: あれは‥‥そうだなあ‥‥。 オイ、何時だった？
English: Hmm... I suppose that would have been around... Hey, what time was it!?

Japanese: え！　あ、あの。 １０時半すぎ‥‥たぶん‥‥
English: Huh!? Umm... I think it was around... I'd say a bit after 10:30 PM... I think...

Japanese: 練習が終わった１０時から、ずっと 入り口のところにいたわけですか？
English: Practice ended at 10 PM so you hung around the lodging house the entire time?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうなりま‥‥すね‥‥ I... I... I...
English: I guess that sounds about right...

Japanese: あの夜もサムかった。 何も用がなかったとは思えません！
English: Wasn't it awfully cold? I can't believe you'd just stand outside in that weather!

Japanese: う。お‥‥じ、じつは‥‥
English: Well... Uh... The truth is...

Japanese: オイ、ギザギザヤロー！ なんだよその言いかたは！
English: Will you shut up, you big nosed dope! Why are you telling him anything extra!?

Japanese: “用”ってなんだよ！ 言ってみろよ！
English: Why can't you believe that we'd just stand outside in that weather?

Japanese: たとえば‥‥だれかを待っていた、 とか。
English: Well, maybe you were waiting for someone?

Japanese: な、な、な、な、な、ななんだと！
English: W-W-W-W-WHAT!? WHO SAID WE WERE WAITING FOR SOMEONE!?

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一！ 推測でモノを言わないで！
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright! We can all do without your off-handed theories!

Japanese: しかし！　この証人は 心なしか、動揺している！
English: But! This witness! He's cracking under the pressure already! I'm on to something!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Hmm... Mr. Wright!

Japanese: この証人が正門前で、だれを 待っていたというのですか？
English: Who do you suppose the witness was waiting for out in the cold that night?

Japanese: この証人が待つとしたら、 １人しかいない！
English: Well, if he was waiting outside in the cold, it was for one person...

Japanese: 猛獣使いのミリカさんです！
English: And one person only! He was waiting for the animal tamer, Regina!

Japanese: ウワワァァッ！
English: WAAAAHHHH!!

Japanese: あなたは、宿舎に帰ってくる ミリカさんを待っていた！
English: You were waiting outside for Regina to come back to the lodging house.

Japanese: ちがいますかッ！
English: Am I mistaken?

Japanese: そうなんですか？
English: Is this true?

Japanese: う。い。イヤ、 ‥‥ボクに聞かれても‥‥
English: Well... I... Umm... You can't really ask me that question...

Japanese: いいじゃねえかよ ダレを待とうが！
English: Who cares who I was waiting for that night!

Japanese: カンジンなのは、オレが 何を見たか、だろうが！
English: What's important is what I saw! Don't you forget it!!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥ たしかにそのとおりです。
English: Well well well... The puppet may be a bit stiff, but he's right.
Eh...

Japanese: 警察が来たのは 何時ぐらいでしたか？
English: What time did the police arrive on the scene would you say?

Japanese: 何度も同じコト、 聞くなって。
English: They could have shown up at any time and nothing would change...

Japanese: １０時半すぎ。 ビタ一文、負けられないぜ！
English: A little after 10:30 PM... There's not a doubt in my mind!

Japanese: （この点については、 ウソは言ってないみたいだ）
English: (It doesn't look like he is lying about this...)

Japanese: いいですか！
English: Alright!

Japanese: この証人は、ちゃんとした 目的があってあそこにいたのです！
English: There is obviously a reason why this witness was there that night!

Japanese: ミリカさんが来るのを ずっと待っていた！
English: He spent all that time waiting for Regina to arrive!
Moreover...

Japanese: 彼女以外のだれかが目の前を 通っても、
English: Even if someone else would've walked right in front of him,

Japanese: 注意を払わなかったはず なのです！
English: I doubt he would have paid them a second thought!

Japanese: ウワワァァッ！
English: AHHHH!!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ たしかにそのとおりで
English: That makes perfect sense.

Japanese: ナニ言ってるの！
English: What did you just say!?

Japanese: 証人はちゃんと、被告人を 目撃しているじゃない！
English: The witness saw the defendant at the scene of the crime!!

Japanese: しかし！　現場に行ったはずの 被害者を目撃していない！
English: However, he did not see the victim on the way to his eventual demise!

Japanese: それならば！
English: If you accept that, then you must accept that there is a high likelihood that...

Japanese: マックス以外の人物を 見逃した可能性だってあるはず
English: He could have missed someone else other than Max heading to the scene!

Japanese: ですイテッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: そもそも、証人が猛獣使いを 待っていたという証拠など、ない！
English: There is absolutely no proof that the witness was waiting for the animal tamer!
Umm... Umm...

Japanese: バレちゃア しょうがねえなあ。
English: I guess you got me...

Japanese: いーよいーよ。教えてやるよ。
English: Alright! Alright! I'll spill the beans for real this time!

Japanese: オレ、ミリカを待ってたんだ！
English: It's true. I was waiting for Regina.

Japanese: わがッ！
English: PAIN!!

Japanese: ‥‥よけいなコトを‥‥！
English: Don't volunteer things!!

Japanese: 証人。ハッキリさせましょう。
English: Mr. Quist, tell us the truth this time... And I mean the whole truth!

Japanese: あなたは、宿舎の入り口で ミリカさんを待っていたんですか？
English: Were you or were you not waiting for Regina at the entrance to the lodging house?

Japanese: ああそうさ！
English: I was!

Japanese: プロポーズしようと思ってさぁ！
English: I was waiting to propose to her!!

Japanese: なな‥‥ぷろぽーず‥‥！
English: You were what!? Waiting to... Propose!?

Japanese: なんだよ！ ベツに、ヒトの勝手だろ。
English: What's the matter!? You think that humans have a monopoly on marriage!?

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥ わた、私は断じて、認めませんぞ！
English: That... The matter of puppet marriage is not under review in this case!

Japanese: ガンコオヤジかてめえは！
English: You're the judge! I mean, look at your horrible outfit!!

Japanese: わがッ！
English: MORE PAIN!!

Japanese: キサマのおかげで、私の カンペキなプランがいきなりパア。
English: Hmph. Thanks to your bumbling, my perfect plan is not so perfect anymore!

Japanese: ‥‥とりあえず、そのふざけた プロポーズにケリをつけましょう。
English: Now we have to waste time getting to the bottom of some silly proposal by a puppet!!

Japanese: ～プロポーズのこと～
English: -- About the Proposal --

Japanese: なんでそんなにサワぐかねェ、 プロポーズぐらいのコトで。
English: Don't be so surprised that I was going to propose to Regina!

Japanese: オレ、ミリカにわたすモノが あったんだよ。
English: I even had something to give to her...

Japanese: ここんとこ、ずっとポッケに入れて チャンスを待ってたんだぜ。
English: I kept it in my pocket, waiting for the chance to propose and give it to her.

Japanese: 当然、あの晩も持っていたわけだ。 ‥‥プレゼントをさ。
English: Of course, I also had it in my pocket that night. It was a present for her.

Japanese: ケッキョクわたせなかったから、 今もまだ持ってるケドな。
English: In the end, I wasn't able to give it to her, so I've still got it in my pocket!

Japanese: ぷろぽーず‥‥あなたが‥‥
English: You were going to propose... You... A puppet...

Japanese: クドいな！ 人形だからってナメんなよな！
English: Don't be so obtuse! Just because I'm a puppet doesn't mean I can't love!

Japanese: はあ‥‥。私ももう若くない、 というコトなんですかねえ‥‥。
English: I guess you're right... Just because I'm old doesn't mean I couldn't propose to her too!

Japanese: そういうこった！
English: Exactly!

Japanese: （裁判長が小さく見えるな‥‥）
English: (His Honor is looking a little less than honorable right now...)

Japanese: じゃ、弁護人。尋問を。 ‥‥はあ。
English: OK Mr. Wright... Please continue with your examination... *sigh*

Japanese: タメ息なんかついてるよ。
English: Aww... Did you hear that? His sigh seemed a little wistful.

Japanese: ～プロポーズのこと～
English: -- About the Proposal --

Japanese: なんでそんなにサワぐかねェ、 プロポーズぐらいのコトで。
English: Don't be so surprised that I was going to propose to Regina!

Japanese: ここんとこ、ずっとポッケに入れて チャンスを待ってたんだぜ。
English: I kept it in my pocket, waiting for the chance to propose and give it to her.

Japanese: 当然、あの晩も持っていたわけだ。 ‥‥プレゼントをさ。
English: Of course, I also had it in my pocket that night. It was a present for her.

Japanese: ケッキョクわたせなかったから、 今もまだ持ってるケドな。
English: In the end, I wasn't able to give it to her, so I've still got it in my pocket!

Japanese: どう？　なるほどくん。
English: How about it Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥そろそろ、ハッキリした ムジュンをつきつけてやろうか。
English: I think it's about time to unwrap this toy's testimony.

Japanese: そうだね。きっと、裁判長も よろこぶと思うよ。
English: That's the spirit Nick! Give him heck!

Japanese: （コンランしてるみたいだな ‥‥裁判長）
English: (Uh oh, the judge has that dazed look again. Maybe he should get out more.)

Japanese: オレ、ミリカにわたすモノが あったんだよ。
English: I even had something to give to her...

Japanese: ミリカにさ。エンゲージ・リングを わたすつもりだったんだ、オレ。
English: I planned on giving an engagement ring to Regina...

Japanese: プロポーズってことはその、 ミリカさんと、ケッコンを‥‥？
English: By proposal, you mean proposing marriage, correct? To Regina.

Japanese: あったりまえだろ！
English: Of course that's what I meant! What kinda stupid question is that!?

Japanese: マンザイのコンビを組むのたぁ ワケがちがうんだぜ！
English: I wasn't going to propose that we become some sort of outlaw biker gang together!!

Japanese: なあ、ベン！
English: Right, Ben!?

Japanese: う。あ‥‥お。
English: Ye-Yeah...

Japanese: ‥‥ってワケさあ！ とにかく！
English: Got it!? That's the truth!

Japanese: “わたすもの” ‥‥なんだったんですか？
English: What was it exactly that you planned on giving her?

Japanese: キマってんだろ！
English: You know exactly what I was going to give her, numbskull!

Japanese: ミリカにピッタリの、 とびきりキレイなヤツだぜ！
English: The only thing I could find that could match Regina's beauty!

Japanese: だからいったい、 それはなんだったの！
English: Answer his question! What was it!?

Japanese: 聞いておどろくな！ ‥‥エンゲージリングさ！
English: You're gonna die when you hear this... It's an engagement ring!!

Japanese: リング！
English: エンゲージ！　<Judge> An Engagement Ring!?!?

Japanese: （２人とも、コシが抜けるほど おどろいてるな‥‥）
English: (Wow... Those two nearly fell out of their chairs!)

Japanese: ふふ‥‥ふざけないで！
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright's joke has gone too far!

Japanese: もういい！　こんな茶番は たくさんよ！
English: Time for this to end right here!

Japanese: （狩魔検事のムチがフキツに うねうねしている‥‥）
English: (Franziska's whip looks like it's about to lash out at almost anything...)

Japanese: （キツい一発をカクゴしても、 ここはこだわるべきか‥‥？）
English: (One hit from that thing will probably shut someone up for a long time...)

Japanese: （ここは勇気を持って、 やめておくか‥‥）
English: (I think I'm gonna live to fight another day and shut up now...)

Japanese: あーあ。みっともないなあ なるほどくん。
English: Nick! Don't be such a coward!

Japanese: 茶番どころか、 これはレキシ的瞬間です！
English: It may be something of a joke, but this is a historic moment!

Japanese: なにしろ、人形が人間に ケッコンを申しこむという‥‥
English: The first time that a puppet has ever proposed to a human being...

Japanese: いてッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥ギロンをスリかえないで。
English: I advise you to cut this argument short...

Japanese: いや。私も弁護人の意見に サンセイです。
English: I'm going to have to agree with the defense here.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。証言を 修正してください。
English: Will the witness please revise their testimony...

Japanese: その‥‥コンヤクゆびわの くだりを。
English: Specifically about the engagement ring... I'd like to stick to facts, not sociology.

Japanese: はああ‥‥。コマかい 男どもだなあマッタク！
English: You sure do enjoy sweating the details, especially for a man in a black bath robe...

Japanese: ポッケ‥‥って、だれの？
English: Who's pocket was the ring in?

Japanese: オレのポッケに 決まってんだろ！
English: Mine of course! What a stupid question!!

Japanese: カザリじゃねえんだよコイツァ！ ‥‥ナメんな！
English: You've got to be kidding me! You think Ben could pull that off!?

Japanese: ど、どうも‥‥スミマセン！ スミマセン！
English: I-I'm s-s-sorry... R-R-Really...

Japanese: あやまるこたァねえよ！ ワルいのはあっちだ！
English: You don't have to apologize for that!! He's the one who should be apologizing!!

Japanese: （そうなのか‥‥？）
English: (Really?)

Japanese: それをわたすために、 宿舎の入り口で？
English: So you went to the lodging house to give it to her?

Japanese: まあな！
English: That's right!

Japanese: 練習中に何度もキッカケを 探したんだけど、
English: I tried to give it to her during practice so many times that I lost count, but...

Japanese: あのキザなクズヤローが ジャマでさあ！
English: That uppity snob kept getting in the way!

Japanese: （このマックス・ギャラクティカを “キザなクズ”だとお！）
English: Uppity snob!? He couldn't possibly be talking about me... Maximillion Galactica!

Japanese: （今度、胴体を切断してやるぞお！ もちろん、タネなしでだッ！）
English: When I get a hold of him, I'm gonna saw his wood block in half... And not with magic!!

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥よくある コイのサヤアテということで。
English: Well... They always say that love creates rivalries.

Japanese: ‥‥はああ。 それで、そのプレゼントは‥‥？
English: So what about this present?

Japanese: ‥‥まだ、ミリカさんに わたそうと‥‥？
English: So you are still thinking of trying to give it to Regina...?

Japanese: あったりまえだろ！
English: Of course I am!

Japanese: なんせ、ギャラ３カ月ぶんだぞ！ カンタンにあきらめられるかッ！
English: I spent three months salary on this thing! I'm not gonna give up that easily!

Japanese: いったい、いくら もらってるのかな？　リロくん。
English: Hmm, I wonder how much he receives for appearing in the circus?

Japanese: ５００円ぐらいじゃないか？
English: Probably way more than he deserves.

Japanese: エンゲージリング‥‥？
English: An engagement ring...?

Japanese: おう！　本格的なガラスで できたダイヤモンドなんだぜ。
English: Uh huh! It's actually a diamond shaped stone cut from glass!

Japanese: ホンモノよりピカピカなんだ！ ミリカもよろこぶと思うなあ。
English: Even more brilliant than the real thing! I think Regina is going to love it!

Japanese: （ゆびわ‥‥ねえ）
English: (It's just a ring...)

Japanese: どうしたの、なるほどくん？
English: What's the matter, Nick?

Japanese: うん‥‥。なんか、 ちょっと引っかかるんだよ‥‥。
English: Well... There's got to be something I can catch him on...

Japanese: ちょっといいですか、証人。
English: Trilo, do you mind if I show you something?

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか。 なんだよ！
English: What is it? What are you talking about!?

Japanese: （うう‥‥やりにくい）
English: (Uh oh... Looks like they're going to double team me now...)

Japanese: こいつに見おぼえは？
English: Do you recognize this ring?

Japanese: あっ！　そ、それ‥‥
English: Ah! That's... That's...

Japanese: オレのじゃねえか！ 返せ！　ドロボー！
English: That's mine!! Give it back! Thief! Thief!

Japanese: さきほど、あなたはこう証言した。
English: Didn't you just testify about this very object? I believe you said...

Japanese: “わたせなかったから、 今もまだ持っている”‥‥と。
English: "In the end, I wasn't able to give it to her, so I've still got it in my pocket!"

Japanese: それがなぜ、 ぼくの手にあるんですか！
English: Why then, do I have it right here...?

Japanese: うがわわアアッ！
English: AAAAAAAAAARRRRRGHHHHH!!

Japanese: どういうことですかッ！
English: What is going on here!?

Japanese: ‥‥そ、それは‥‥
English: That's... That's...

Japanese: おい、ベン！　出番だ。
English: Ben! Say something!!

Japanese: え！　こ、困るよリロくん‥‥
English: Eh? Don't put me on the spot like that Trilo!!

Japanese: ぼくはこいつを、ルーサーの ウチで見つけました。
English: I found this in Money's room...

Japanese: るーさーさん‥‥？ どなたですか、そのかたは。
English: M-M-Money's room? You mean a room they put money, like a bank vault?

Japanese: ハッ！　うすぎたねえ サルだよヤツぁ！
English: Hah! That filthy monkey is gonna get what's coming to him!!

Japanese: 証人。‥‥ちょっと、 口が悪すぎますよ。
English: Mr. Quist, I would prefer if you avoided slandering innocent fiats in my court.

Japanese: ルーサーはその‥‥ ホントにサルなんですけど。
English: Well, Your Honor... Money really is a monkey... In every sense of the word.

Japanese: あ。そ、そうなんですか。
English: Ah. I see... Well then...

Japanese: ルーサーは、光るモノを 集めるクセがあるのです。
English: Money likes to go after the shiniest things that he can find and gather them up.

Japanese: ひ‥‥光るもの‥‥？
English: Shiny... Things?

Japanese: 証人！
English: Trilo!

Japanese: このリングは、 いつ盗まれたのですか！
English: When was this ring stolen from you?

Japanese: ‥‥そ‥‥それは‥‥
English: Well... I suppose it was...

Japanese: ‥‥あのとき‥‥ 事件があった、夜‥‥です‥‥
English: That time... You know... That night... The night of the crime...

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: What did you just say!?

Japanese: もっとくわしく！
English: Details... I need more details...

Japanese: ‥‥マックスさんが‥‥広場に 入っていった‥‥すぐあとです‥‥
English: Well... It was stolen right after Max showed up in the plaza...

Japanese: あなたの目の前を、被告が 通りすぎたときですか！
English: Right about when you saw the defendant walk past, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥え、あ‥‥う。ええ。 その、すぐあとに‥‥
English: Well... Umm... I guess you... Might... Umm... Be able to say that...

Japanese: リングをその。あの。ながめて いたら‥‥
English: The ring might have... Well... It could have been... Taken around that time...

Japanese: キーッ！
English: Oooo-Ooooh!

Japanese: ギャアアアアアアアアアアアアッ
English: Ahhhhh... Yeowwwwwwwww... Ohhhhhhhhhh...

Japanese: それがなんだってんだよ！
English: Ben! What's with you!? Oh, whatever...

Japanese: カンケーねえだろ！ そんなコト、事件とはさあ！
English: It has nothing to do with anything! Especially not who committed the murder!!

Japanese: ‥‥ところが、 そうはいかないのです。
English: It's not for you to decide what has to do with what!

Japanese: 証人！
English: Now Trilo! Back to the topic at hand!

Japanese: オレはショーニンじゃねえ！ リロくんだ！
English: I haven't admitted a thing! Not I... Mr. Trilo Quist!

Japanese: リングを取られたリロくんは、 どうしましたか！
English: What did you do when the ring was taken Trilo?

Japanese: 決まってんだろ！ 追いかけたさ、あのサルを！
English: You know exactly what I did! I chased after that ring snatching monkey Money!!

Japanese: でも、追いつけなかった‥‥？
English: But you weren't able to catch up with him were you?

Japanese: このベンのヤツがさあ、 トロすぎたんだよ！
English: It's all this slow loafy fool called Ben's fault!

Japanese: 雪でコロんでモガモガやってる あいだに、逃げられちまった。
English: While he was fumbling his way through the snow, that dumb monkey was able to get away!

Japanese: それはそれは。 タイヘンでしたねえ‥‥。
English: That is indeed an incredible shame.

Japanese: ‥‥しかしこれで、 １つ証明されたことがあります。
English: Well... This does indeed prove one very important point.

Japanese: しょ‥‥証明されたこと‥‥？ な、何よそれはッ！
English: Prove an important point!? What point could that possibly be?

Japanese: それは！　この証人が 運動不足だ、ということです！
English: Obviously that one Mr. Benjamin Woodman is lacking in the area of exercise!!

Japanese: ‥‥だって、そうでしょう！
English: This is clear from the testimony we have just listened to!!

Japanese: 雪に足を取られて、 サルをとり逃がすなんてッ‥‥！
English: He was too slow and awkward to catch a monkey fleeing through the snow!!

Japanese: （‥‥なんかさっきから、ヤケに みんなの視線がとおいな‥‥）
English: (This revelation doesn't seem to be registering too well around the courtroom...)

Japanese: 引いちゃってるんだよ、みんな！ ホラ！　考えなおそうよ！
English: Maybe you should leave the courtroom drama for video games and rethink that one...

Japanese: う‥‥うん。
English: Umm... OK.

Japanese: この証人の証言には、 大きなムジュンがある！
English: There is a huge contradiction in this witness' testimony!

Japanese: ム‥‥ムジュン！
English: Co-Contradiction!?

Japanese: さっき、証人はこう証言しました！
English: The witness just testified to the following effect--

Japanese: “警察が来るまで、広場の 入り口から動かなかった”と。
English: Up until the police arrived, he didn't move from the entrance to the plaza.

Japanese: しかし！　証人はサルを 追いかけていた！
English: HOWEVER! The witness just stated that he chased after Money the monkey.

Japanese: そのあいだ、入り口には だれもいなかったことになる！
English: When the witness was off chasing Money, there was no one watching the plaza!

Japanese: なんですって！
English: What is the meaning of all this, Mr. Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: だ、だから‥‥なんだと言うの！ 成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: Where are you going with this little theory of yours?

Japanese: この証人は、ずっと入り口を 見ていたわけではない‥‥
English: I'm saying that there is no possible way that this witness saw the plaza the entire time!

Japanese: ということは、つまり！
English: That's where I'm going with this little theory! Which leads me to my next point.

Japanese: 被告人以外の人物が、 現場に行った可能性がある！
English: It is entirely possible that someone other than the defendant was at the scene!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう
English: Interesting Mr. Wright.

Japanese: じゃ‥‥じゃあ聞かせて！ 成歩堂　龍一！
English: Well then tell me this, Mr. Phoenix Wright!!

Japanese: この人形使いが、だれかを 見落とした証拠でも、あるの！
English: Do you have any proof that something slipped past this vigilant ventriloquist?

Japanese: ジッサイに、被害者の立見団長を 見落としてるじゃないですか！
English: Well, he obviously didn't see the victim, the Ringmaster, arrive on the scene!

Japanese: しかし！　ハッキリ被告人を 目撃しているジジツは変わらない！
English: However, that doesn't change the fact that he saw the defendant arrive!

Japanese: この証人はウソをついていた！ それに、被告に悪意も抱いている！
English: The witness is lying! He is blinded by his rivalry with Max!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥ 弁護人の意見には一理あります。
English: Well... The defense's argument does hold water.

Japanese: たしかにこの証人は、マックスさん に敵意があるようですからねえ。
English: This witness does have a history of animosity towards the defendant.

Japanese: ‥‥な‥‥なんだよそれ！
English: What!? How dare you!?

Japanese: オレはそんなケツの穴が ちっちゃい男じゃねえぞ！
English: I wouldn't lie just to get that dorkface in trouble! He's not even worth it!!

Japanese: 見たんだって！　キザでクズな インチキヤローを！
English: I saw him! No doubt about it! I saw that worthless liar!

Japanese: 念のため、目撃した人物のことを、 もう少し証言してもらいましょう。
English: Well... Just for clarity's sake, let's flesh out exactly who you saw on that night.

Japanese: ハッ！　何度やっても 同じコトだと思うけどな！
English: Hah! I've told you so many times, you'd think you'd know my story's not changing!

Japanese: （そう思うのが、人形の浅はかさ ってトコだな‥‥）
English: (You've already changed your story stick boy! And I'm sure it will change some more.)

Japanese: １つのウソは、 必ず次のウソを生み出す‥‥。
English: Where there is one lie, there are usually many more behind it.

Japanese: そういうことだね。 一気に追いつめてやろうぜ！
English: Exactly, Maya. That's why we have to keep after him!

Japanese: うん！
English: Yeah!

Japanese: ～マックスを目撃したこと～
English: -- Witnessing Max --

Japanese: ミリカのコト待ってたのは 認めてやるぜ。
English: I'll give you that I was waiting that night for Regina.

Japanese: でも、まちがいなく ヤツは広場に入っていったんだ。
English: But that doesn't change the fact that I saw Max in the plaza that night!

Japanese: あそこに行ってから、 ５分ほど待ってたころだったぜ。
English: He showed up after I had been waiting there for about five minutes.

Japanese: こんばんは、ってアイサツしたのに アイツ、ムシしやがって！
English: I said "good evening" to him, but he didn't even acknowledge my presence!

Japanese: とにかくアイツ、あのマックス だったんだよ。まちがいねえや！
English: I'm absolutely sure it was him! I saw Maximillion Galactica at the scene!!

Japanese: キザなシンボル３点セットを、 オレはまちがいなく見たしな！
English: There's no way I could mistake someone wearing those three ridiculous symbols!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: このあと、サルのルーサーが 現れた、というワケね。
English: So that means that Money didn't show up until after you saw Max?

Japanese: そうさ。アイツを見てから １分もしなかったな。
English: That's right. Money ran up less than a minute after I saw Max.

Japanese: そしてリングをうばわれて、 そのあとを追跡した‥‥。
English: Then Money snatched the ring, and you went chasing after him?

Japanese: もどってくるまでに、 どれぐらいかかりましたか？
English: How long was it until you came back to where you were waiting?

Japanese: さあな。どれぐらいだった？
English: Well. Let's see... I'd say about...

Japanese: たぶん‥‥５分ぐらい、 だった‥‥と。思いますが‥‥
English: I suppose five minutes. I think...

Japanese: 被害者は、その５分のあいだに 現場に行ったのよ。
English: So the victim could have arrived on the scene in that five minute stretch.

Japanese: ‥‥では、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Mr. Wright, please proceed with your cross-examination.

Japanese: ～マックスを目撃したこと～
English: -- Witnessing Max --

Japanese: ミリカのコト待ってたのは 認めてやるぜ。
English: I'll give you that I was waiting that night for Regina.

Japanese: でも、まちがいなく ヤツは広場に入っていったんだ。
English: But that doesn't change the fact that I saw Max in the plaza that night!

Japanese: あそこに行ってから、 ５分ほど待ってたころだったぜ。
English: He showed up after I had been waiting there for about five minutes.

Japanese: こんばんは、ってアイサツしたのに アイツ、ムシしやがって！
English: I said "good evening" to him, but he didn't even acknowledge my presence!

Japanese: とにかくアイツ、あのマックス だったんだよ。まちがいねえや！
English: I'm absolutely sure it was him! I saw Maximillion Galactica at the scene!!

Japanese: キザなシンボル３点セットを、 オレはまちがいなく見たしな！
English: There's no way I could mistake someone wearing those three ridiculous symbols!

Japanese: うーん‥‥どうも 気になる部分があるよね。証言。
English: There has to be a hole in his testimony somewhere.

Japanese: そうだね。 だけど‥‥証拠がなあ‥‥
English: I agree. But do we have the proof to make something stick?

Japanese: でもさ。ここまできたら、 やってみるしかないって！
English: All we can do is try, right Nick!?

Japanese: たたきつけちゃおうよ、 証拠。‥‥とりあえずさ！
English: We've gotta have something that will prove useful.

Japanese: “とりあえず”か‥‥
English: All right, time to go to work.

Japanese: ミリカさんのことばかり 気にしていて‥‥、
English: So you were only concerned with waiting for Regina that night?

Japanese: だれかベツの人物がとおるのを 見落とした、ということは？
English: That means you probably wouldn't have noticed if someone else showed up.

Japanese: あのなあ。オレ、いくつ 目がついてると思ってるワケ？
English: You should think about how many eyes I actually have.

Japanese: ‥‥４つよ、４つ。 ベンのと合わせて。
English: I've got four, you know! Four. F-O-U-R. Counting Ben of course...

Japanese: 見落とすワケ、ねえだろうが！
English: With that many eyes, do you really think something would have slipped by me?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥それはたしかに、 そうかもしれませんねえ‥‥。
English: Four eyes is an awful lot of attention directed at one area, I suppose...

Japanese: （リロくんの証言より、裁判長の 発言のほうがモンダイだな‥‥）
English: (Yikes! The judge is even more dangerous to our case than Trilo!)

Japanese: その人物は、たしかに テントの中から出てきたんですか？
English: So you saw Max coming out of the Big Top that night?

Japanese: ハッ！　あたりめえだろ！
English: Of course that's where I saw him coming from!

Japanese: オレたち、ずっとテントの 入り口を見てたんだぜ！
English: I was staring at the entrance to the tent the entire time!

Japanese: （そりゃそうか。ミリカちゃんを 待ってたんだもんな‥‥）
English: (I guess that makes sense, especially since he was waiting for Regina.)

Japanese: それは、時間でいうと、 何時ごろのことですか？
English: About what time would you say these events took place?

Japanese: バカかオマエ。そんなケーサンも できねえのかよ！
English: You're one of the dumbest people on the planet if you can't figure it out yourself!

Japanese: くだらねえ練習が終わったのが １０時ピッタシ！
English: You already know that practice finished promptly at 10 PM!

Japanese: くだらねえ宿舎の入り口に 行ったのが、その５分後！
English: And you already know that I went to the lodging house right after practice!

Japanese: それから５分して、 オレたちはヤツを見た。
English: You don't need to be a brain surgeon to know around what time it was when I saw him...

Japanese: ‥‥ホレ、何時だ？
English: Just add ten more minutes! I'm sure you can do that! Now what time was it?

Japanese: 何時ですか？
English: Indeed, what time was it?

Japanese: 何時でしょう？
English: Hmm... What time was it? Lemme think about that for a second.........

Japanese: だってケーサンはニガテだ
English: ...YEOW!! I'm no good at math!
*sigh* It was 10:10 PM!

Japanese: そ、そういうことですね。
English: Ahhh, yes. That sounds about right!

Japanese: とにかく、そのときヤツが 来たワケよ！
English: It sounds about right because that's the time that I saw Max on the scene!

Japanese: “こんばんは”って アイサツしたんですか？
English: So you testified that you said "good evening" to Max that night.

Japanese: なーにバカみたいなコト 言ってんだよ！
English: You must enjoy asking incredibly obvious questions!

Japanese: 朝は“おはよう”！ ヒルは“こんにちは”！
English: You say "good morning" in the morning, and "good afternoon" during the day, right?

Japanese: そして夜は“おやすみ”！ ‥‥だろ？
English: And it's obvious that I'd say "good night" to someone at night!!

Japanese: おい、そこでツッコミだろベン！
English: What Ben!? You've got something to add!?

Japanese: “ちがうよリロくん、夜は 《こんばんは》だよ”ってさあ！
English: Lemme guess... "That's not it Trilo! You say 'good evening' at night!"

Japanese: あ‥‥ご、ゴメン。 リロくん‥‥。
English: Ah... I'm sorry Trilo...

Japanese: 証人！　こんなところで 芸の練習をしないように。
English: Mr. Quist, I would prefer if you kept your ventriloquist's act outside of the courtroom!

Japanese: 芸人は、生きザマすべてが 芸なんだよ！　シロートがッ！
English: Impossible! A performer lives and breathes his performances! You should know better!!

Japanese: （この証言‥‥なんか、 おかしなところはないか？）
English: (There's got to be something wrong with this bit of testimony...)

Japanese: （人の芸にクチを出せるほど エラくないからな‥‥）
English: (I guess it's kinda petty to bad mouth someone's performances...)

Japanese: だよねー。なんたって、 “やめなシャレ”だもん。
English: I think so too... I guess you really are "Wright all the time!"

Japanese: 一生、言われそうだなあ ‥‥それ。
English: Glad to see you finally coming around to that.

Japanese: 証人。証言をつづけなさい。
English: Continue with the cross-examination please!

Japanese: ちょっとフシギなんですが‥‥。
English: Isn't that a bit strange to you?

Japanese: なんだよ？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: そもそもリロくんが、 マックスにアイサツするなんて、
English: Well, if you hate Max so much, why would you bother being nice to him?

Japanese: オカシイと思うんですけど。
English: It strikes me as somewhat strange.

Japanese: ど、どういうコトだよ？
English: Why would it strike you as strange?

Japanese: 仲間にアイサツをして、 ナニがおかしいの！
English: Exactly! How is it strange to be cordial to one of your coworkers?

Japanese: いや、だって‥‥
English: Well... If it was simply just being cordial to a coworker I would understand...

Japanese: いてっ！
English: OWW!! That hurt!!

Japanese: ごちゃごちゃ言う前に、 証拠を見せなさい！
English: Maybe you should think of having some proof before your lips start flapping next time!

Japanese: この社会は証拠がすべて。 ‥‥学校で習わなかったの！
English: Proof is everything in this world! You should have learned that back in grade school!!

Japanese: （“リロくんが、マックスに アイサツするわけがない”）
English: (There's no reason that Trilo would ever say something nice to Max...)

Japanese: （‥‥そんなコト、 証拠で立証できるのかな‥‥）
English: (But how do I go about proving that with the evidence?)

Japanese: この社会はハッタリがすべて。 ‥‥学校で習ったでしょ！
English: Bluffing is everything in this world! ...But I'm sure you already learned that one!!

Japanese: やってみるしかない、か‥‥。
English: I guess I can give it a shot...

Japanese: 証人。証言にもどりなさい。
English: The witness will resume his testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥今のリロくんのボケに、 そのツッコミはおかしい！
English: I hate to say it, but Ben's part of your little act is coming off a bit stale!

Japanese: ツッコミのプロに 言わせれば、そこはむしろ‥‥
English: I'm sure that if a pro was to review your work, he would say...

Japanese: いてっ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: “ツッコミのプロ”って アナタのこと？
English: You're not trying to pass yourself off as a professional performer, are you?

Japanese: 調子に乗るな！ 成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: Don't get so wrapped up in yourself, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: このッ！　ナルホドーがッ！
English: You! Should know! Better!

Japanese: いてッ！　いたたたッ！
English: OW! OWWW! OWWWWWW! THAT HURTS!!

Japanese: ‥‥スッキリした。 証人。証言をつづけなさい。
English: That should have set him straight! You may proceed with your testimony, Witness.

Japanese: ハッキリ自信を持って 言えますか？
English: You are 100% absolutely sure of what you just said?

Japanese: さっきから言ってるだろ！ ハッキリ自信を持ってよお！
English: I told you already! Jeez!! I am 100% absolutely sure!

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ、それほど カクシンがあるの？
English: There's no way that he could be any more confident than that, right?

Japanese: キザな３点セット‥‥？
English: Those three ridiculous symbols, huh?

Japanese: ‥‥オマエ、それ聞くの 何回目だよ！
English: Out of curiosity, exactly how many times have you asked that question by now?

Japanese: しかたねえなあ！ さあ、みなさんごいっしょに！
English: If you're gonna ask again, I'll answer with a chorus... Everyone together now!

Japanese: “シルクハット！<Maya> マント！ 白いバラ！”
English: "SILK HAT! CLOAK! WHITE ROSES!"

Japanese: ‥‥センキウ！
English: Thank you very much!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんまで‥‥
English: Maya... You didn't have to join in did you!?

Japanese: だって、楽しそうだったから。
English: But... It's fun shouting out with everyone else!!

Japanese: 証人。あなたは事件当日、 マックスとケンカしましたね？
English: Trilo, is it not true that you had a fight with Max on the day of the murder?

Japanese: ‥‥ケンカ？
English: A fight?

Japanese: ミリカさんをめぐって。
English: A fight over Regina to be exact.

Japanese: そんな大したモンじゃねえよ。 ちょっとした口ゲンカ‥‥。
English: It wasn't that big of a deal! It was just an argument... A disagreement at most!

Japanese: 口ゲンカでは コイツは割れません。
English: A disagreement usually doesn't end with someone getting clonked over the head...

Japanese: うがわわアアッ！
English: AHHHH!!

Japanese: あの日の朝、コイツでマックスに 思いっきり殴られましたね？
English: That morning, Ben got clobbered over the head by Max, didn't he?

Japanese: な、なんですって！
English: Wh-What?

Japanese: それは‥‥ リッパな傷害罪ですぞ！
English: Is that an admission of assault and battery?

Japanese: ほほろッ！
English: OUCH!!

Japanese: ‥‥それ以前に、被告人は 殺人罪で裁かれているわ。
English: Before we handle that, we should wrap up the defendant's murder charge first!

Japanese: 事件当日、被告と証人は 大ゲンカをしていた！
English: The truth is that on the day of the crime, the defendant and witness had a huge fight!

Japanese: しかも、カンタンに 仲なおりできる問題ではない！
English: There is absolutely no way they would have suddenly become cordial that evening!

Japanese: それに、この証人の性格を 考えてください！
English: Moreover, just consider the personality of the witness on stand!

Japanese: “こんばんは”なんてアイサツ、 するワケがないッ！
English: There is no way a puppet this lewd would just up and say "good evening" to his rival!

Japanese: ぐばあああっ！
English: GWWAAAAHHHH!!

Japanese: じゃあ、この証人が ウソをついているというの！
English: Are you saying this witness is lying!?

Japanese: 罪を着せるために “被告を見た”と証言したと！
English: That he is trying to frame the defendant by claiming to have seen him at the crime scene!?

Japanese: オ‥‥オレ、ウソなんか ついていねえぞ！　だって‥‥
English: I-I-I... I didn't tell a single lie! Honestly! I just...

Japanese: そこまで！ ‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: That's enough from you Mr. Quist! Mr. Wright...

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: では、うかがいましょう。
English: Let's clarify this testimony for the court...

Japanese: この証人の“被告を見た”という 証言を、どう考えますか？
English: Could you explain your theory about who the witness actually saw that night?

Japanese: もちろん証人は、 だれも見ていないのです！
English: Of course, the witness did not see a soul that night.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: なな、なんでそんなコト 決めつけるんだよ！
English: Who... Who do you think you are to just up and decide what it is I saw that night!?

Japanese: バカのバカはバカにしか リカイできない‥‥。
English: A fool can only understand a foolish foolery uttered by the most foolish of fools.

Japanese: この証人が “だれも見ていない”‥‥
English: This witness "didn't see anyone"...

Japanese: そんな証拠はあるのッ！
English: You wouldn't happen to have proof to back up that statement, would you?

Japanese: うぐ‥‥！
English: *gulp*

Japanese: なるほどくん、 あたし、思うんだけど。
English: Nick, I just thought of something...

Japanese: もし本当にベンさんたちが、 だれも見ていないんだったら、
English: If Ben and Trilo really didn't see anyone that night,

Japanese: “アイサツをした”なんて証言、 しないんじゃないかな？
English: they wouldn't have testified that they greeted someone that night, would they?

Japanese: ‥‥ど、どういうこと？
English: Wh-What do you mean?

Japanese: リロくんたちはきっと、ホントに “こんばんは”って言ったんだよ。
English: I think that Trilo really did say "good evening" to someone that night.

Japanese: でも‥‥あの２人は ケンカしてたんだぞ‥‥？
English: But... Trilo and Max... They were in the middle of a huge fight.

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、考えたほうが いいようですな。
English: Maybe you should think this over some more on your own time, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: もちろん、被告を見たんです。 マックス・ギャラクティカをね！
English: Of course he saw the defendant! How could he miss Maximillion Galactica!?

Japanese: うわっ！
English: OWWWW!!

Japanese: “マックスに会ったのなら、 アイサツするハズがない”
English: You said that Trilo would have no reason to greet Max if they had met that night!

Japanese: ‥‥そう言いだしたのは アナタでしょ！　成歩堂　龍一！
English: You said that, did you not!? Mr. Phoenix Wright!!
Ahh...

Japanese: “あ”じゃないッ！
English: "Ahh..." is not the correct answer!

Japanese: 弁護人！　今の“あ”には、 重大なペナルティを与えます！
English: Mr. Wright! I'm afraid that "Ahh" has just landed the defense a penalty!

Japanese: （しまった‥‥）
English: (Foiled again...)

Japanese: も、もう一度、考えなおそうよ！
English: Nick, you've got to think about things a bit more and give it another shot!

Japanese: う‥‥うん。 （それも、大急ぎで‥‥な！）
English: Yeah, I know... (And by the looks of things I've gotta hurry up!)

Japanese: たしかに証人は、 だれかを見たのでしょう。
English: It is my belief that the witness did indeed see someone that night...

Japanese: だから“こんばんは”と アイサツしたのです。
English: It was just someone else! That's who he said "good evening" to.

Japanese: ‥‥考えるまでもない！
English: What kind of theory is that!?

Japanese: ただし。
English: The correct one.

Japanese: 証人はその人物を、 マックスだとは思わなかった。
English: Furthermore, I don't believe the person the witness saw was Max at all.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: マックスに会ったのなら、 アイサツをするわけがない。
English: If he had truly met Max that night, there would have been no greeting at all.

Japanese: ‥‥とすれば、答えは１つ。
English: Which means there is only one proper answer.

Japanese: 証人が見たのは マックスではなかった。
English: The person the witness saw that night was not Maximillion Galactica.

Japanese: だから、リロくんは その人物にアイサツをしたのです。
English: That is why Trilo made the effort to greet whoever it was that he saw that evening.

Japanese: “こんばんは”‥‥とね。
English: Or "good evening" as he put it.
Uhh...

Japanese: いったい‥‥それは‥‥
English: What in the world... You...

Japanese: 証人が“アイサツ”した人物‥‥ それは、だれだったのですか？
English: Would the defense kindly explain who it was Trilo saw that evening then?

Japanese: 証人は、この人物だと思って アイサツしたのです！
English: This is the person that this witness felt the need to greet that night!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ いかがですか？　証人。
English: Hmm... How about it, Mr. Quist?

Japanese: いかがもワキガもあるかよ！
English: How about you stop asking me these ridiculous questions!

Japanese: デタラメな証拠を見せられて、 それこそゴアイサツ、ってもんだ！
English: You can show me all the crappy pictures you'd like, there's not one I wouldn't greet!

Japanese: ナニやってんだよベン！
English: What the oak are you doing, Woodman?

Japanese: そこですかさず“それはちがうよ リロくん”だろ！
English: You better not say, "Trilo watch your mouth!"

Japanese: あ。ご、ゴメンよリロくん‥‥
English: Ahh... Well... OK. Sorry about that, Trilo.

Japanese: もっとしっかりしなさい！ 弁護人！
English: Order! ORDER! There will be order in my courtroom! Mr. Wright!!

Japanese: （ぼくまで怒られた‥‥）
English: (Uh oh, now I've done it!)

Japanese: もう一度、考えなおしてください。
English: You better rethink this line of questioning before I lose my patience with you!!

Japanese: 性格のワルい証人が、わざわざ アイサツする人物は、ただ１人。
English: Considering the ill temper of this witness, there is only one person he would greet!

Japanese: もちろん、 ミリカさんですね！
English: It must be Regina! It's Regina right!? She's so cute!!

Japanese: い‥‥いえ、ちがいます。
English: No, Your Honor, it is not Regina.

Japanese: もしそうなら、ゆびわを プレゼントしていたはずですから。
English: If it was Regina, Trilo would have given her the engagement ring as a present.

Japanese: あ。それもそうですね‥‥。
English: Oh... Yeah... I suppose you've got a point there.

Japanese: ‥‥立見　七百人。 被害者自身、というわけ？
English: It was Russell Berry. The victim himself, was it not?

Japanese: もちろん、そのとおり！
English: You are correct. It was indeed Russell Berry.

Japanese: あなたが見た人物は、 被害者の団長だった！
English: The person you saw that evening was the victim, the Ringmaster, Russell Berry.

Japanese: だからあなたはアイサツした。 ‥‥そうですね！
English: That's why you greeted him Trilo. Isn't that correct?
*gulp*

Japanese: どうなんですか、リロくん！
English: Answer the question, Mr. Quist!

Japanese: うああああああああッ！
English: WAAAAHHHH!! AH!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥どういうことですか、これは！
English: ORDER! ORDER! How do you respond to this!?

Japanese: ま、待ってくれよ！
English: Wa-Wait a second!!

Japanese: たしかに、最初は オヤジかと思ったんだ！
English: Well, at first I thought it was the old man!

Japanese: でも！　でも！　よく見たら、 やっぱりマックスだったんだッ！
English: But! But! Once I got a better look at him, it was obviously Maximillion Galactica!

Japanese: ここで一度、 問題を整理してみましょう。
English: I think it is high time that we clear the air about this question!

Japanese: 証人は、現場である広場の入り口で １人の人物を目撃しています。
English: Mr. Quist obviously witnessed a single person in the area of the plaza that evening.

Japanese: 問題は、それはいったい だれだったのか？
English: The problem is identifying exactly who that person was...

Japanese: マックス・ギャラクティカか？ 団長の立見　七百人だったのか？
English: Was it Maximillion Galactica? Or was it the Ringmaster, one Mr. Russell Berry?

Japanese: 検察側は、被告人だったと 主張するわ！
English: The prosecution argues that it was the defendant that the witness saw that night!

Japanese: ジッサイに証人は、被告の シンボルを３つ、目撃している！
English: The witness has clearly stated that he saw the defendant's three symbols!

Japanese: シンボルを３つ‥‥？
English: Three symbols?

Japanese: まだおぼえてねえのかよ！
English: Alright... This is getting old. C'mon man! You've gotta remember them by now!!

Japanese: しかたねえなあ、 みなさん、ごいっしょに‥‥
English: Here we go again! Everyone... All together now!!

Japanese: わははあッ！
English: OWWWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥シルクハット、マント、 そしてムネの白いバラ‥‥。
English: Yes, yes... We know... The silk hat, cloak, and white roses...

Japanese: シルクハットにマント？ そんなモノ、ぼくだって着られる！
English: A silk hat and a cloak! Anyone could wear them! They'd even look good on me!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって‥‥！
English: What was that!?

Japanese: 証人は、さっきから３点セットの コトばかりくりかえしています。
English: Well, the witness has endlessly repeated that he saw Max's three symbols...

Japanese: つまり、それ以外のものは 見ていないのです！
English: However, how do we really know it was Maximillion Galactica?

Japanese: それならば、証人の見た 人影の正体はただ１つ！
English: It could have been someone else, dressed up as him.

Japanese: それは、マックスの衣装を着た 立見　七百人だった！
English: Possibly even Russell Berry!

Japanese: ‥‥ぐぐ‥‥うッ！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: 狩魔検事。
English: Ms. von Karma.

Japanese: 証人が見た人物が被告だったという ハッキリした証拠はありますか？
English: Do you have clear evidence that the person the witness saw was the defendant?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: Well... I...

Japanese: ‥‥それならば、現時点で 判決は下せません。
English: If that's the case, then it is impossible for me to make a judgment at this point.

Japanese: （よし！　なんとか 切りぬけたぞ‥‥！）
English: (Yes! I think we've finally won a point in this one!!)

Japanese: ‥‥それはザンネンね。
English: That is very... Unfortunate.
Huh?

Japanese: 帰りじたくをするのは早いわ。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: You're just a little too excited for your own good, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことだ？
English: What do you mean by that?

Japanese: この証人から聞きたかったのは、 たった１つだけ。
English: You merely established one thing from this witness.

Japanese: “事件の夜、１人の人物が広場に 向かうのを見た”
English: You established that this witness saw one person in the plaza that night.

Japanese: ‥‥その答えは出たわ。 となれば‥‥
English: I applaud you on your effort, but...

Japanese: となれば？
English: But...?

Japanese: 次の証人を呼ぶまで。
English: Who that person was can only be answered by the next witness!

Japanese: な‥‥なにッ！
English: HUH!?

Japanese: 裁判長！　検察側の立証は、 次こそが本番よ！
English: Your Honor, the prosecution will provide, beyond a shadow of a doubt,

Japanese: だれもがナットクする 答えを提示するわ！
English: an answer to that question, and evidence that clearly establishes one thing.

Japanese: マックス・ギャラクティカ以外、 この犯行は不可能だった、と！
English: That there is no one other than Maximillion Galactica responsible for this crime!

Japanese: ‥‥了解しました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: ここで一度、１０分間の 休憩をとりたいと思います。
English: The court will take a 10 minute recess.

Japanese: 検察側は、証人の準備を しておいてください。
English: During that time, I request the prosecution prepare their next witness.

Japanese: ‥‥では、休憩に入ります！
English: Court is now in recess!

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、山田　耕平に この場で、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Billy Bob Johns...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
